evals.prompt: hey can you set up the agentic documentation for this repo? it's a python microservice that handles order processing - we have about 50 files, mostly in src/orders/ and src/payments/. I want to make sure the AI assistant can understand the architecture when helping devs work on it. The repo is at /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/ai-helpers/.work/skill-refinement-create/workspace/test-order-service
evals.expected_output: Should invoke /agentic-docs:create with the repo path, explain the workflow phases clearly, show progress through each phase, handle the structure creation properly, invoke all 12 generation skills, validate output, generate eval suite, and display final metrics summary. Should be clear about what's happening at each step.

assertions.name: mentions_8_phases
assertions.description: Response explains the 8-phase workflow structure

assertions.name: mentions_18_files
assertions.description: Response mentions creating 18 documentation files

assertions.name: provides_time_estimate
assertions.description: Response provides estimated duration for the workflow

assertions.name: explains_orchestrator_pattern
assertions.description: Response explains that this is an orchestrator that invokes sub-skills

assertions.name: mentions_validation_phase
assertions.description: Response mentions validation will occur

evals.prompt: I tried running /agentic-docs:create on my repo but it failed halfway through - something about generate-reliability-md not finding any monitoring config. What do I do now? Do I need to delete everything and start over or can I just regenerate the reliability doc?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the error handling strategy clearly - identify that generate-reliability-md is a non-critical skill, explain that partial docs are preserved, show how to check what was created, offer specific recovery options (re-run entire create, or run just the failed skill individually), reference the log file for debugging. Should NOT tell user to delete partial output.

assertions.name: identifies_non_critical
assertions.description: Response identifies generate-reliability-md failure as non-critical

assertions.name: no_delete_recommendation
assertions.description: Response does NOT recommend deleting partial documentation

assertions.name: provides_recovery_options
assertions.description: Response provides at least 2 specific recovery options with commands

assertions.name: explains_partial_preservation
assertions.description: Response explains that partial documentation is preserved intact

assertions.name: references_critical_vs_noncritical
assertions.description: Response distinguishes between critical and non-critical failures

evals.prompt: we have a really large go monorepo (probably 500+ files) and i need to generate agentic docs for it but i'm in a hurry. is there a faster way to do this? I don't need the evaluation suite right now, just the core documentation
evals.expected_output: Should recommend using --parallel flag for faster execution, explain the parallel mode benefits (40-60% faster), also recommend --skip-evals to skip evaluation generation, show the command with both flags, explain the trade-offs (higher memory usage), provide time estimates (sequential vs parallel)

assertions.name: mentions_skip_evals_flag
assertions.description: Response mentions --skip-evals flag to skip evaluation suite

assertions.name: mentions_parallel_flag
assertions.description: Response mentions --parallel flag for concurrent execution

assertions.name: provides_time_comparison
assertions.description: Response compares execution times for different approaches

assertions.name: explains_tradeoffs
assertions.description: Response explains trade-offs of using performance flags (memory, complexity, etc.)

assertions.name: recommends_both_flags
assertions.description: Response recommends using both --skip-evals and --parallel for large repos

evals.prompt: Can you explain what /agentic-docs:create does and walk me through what would happen if I ran it on a repository?
evals.expected_output: Should handle the terse command-style input, invoke the skill with the provided path, execute the full workflow (all phases 0-8), show clear progress indicators, handle any issues gracefully, display final metrics. Should work just as well as a verbose request.

assertions.name: explains_purpose
assertions.description: Response clearly explains the purpose of agentic-docs:create

assertions.name: describes_workflow_phases
assertions.description: Response walks through the workflow phases in order

assertions.name: lists_output_files
assertions.description: Response lists what documentation files will be created

assertions.name: mentions_duration
assertions.description: Response provides time estimate

assertions.name: well_organized
assertions.description: Response is well-structured with clear sections/headers
